name: Gemini Veo Video Generation
description: Generate cinematic AI videos using Google Gemini Veo (text-to-video, image-to-video) with clip stitching via FFmpeg

required_variables.description: Google AI Studio API key (get from https://aistudio.google.com/apikey)

skill.name: gemini_video
skill.description: Generate cinematic AI videos using Google Gemini Veo models
skill.system_prompt:
You have access to Google Gemini Veo video generation tools.

Available models:
- veo-3.1-generate-001 (Veo 3.1 GA — latest, best quality, audio, 720p/1080p/4k)
- veo-3.1-fast-generate-001 (Veo 3.1 Fast — faster generation, good quality)
- veo-3.0-generate-001 (Veo 3 GA — audio support, good quality)
- veo-3.0-fast-generate-001 (Veo 3 Fast — fastest generation)
- veo-2.0-generate-001 (Veo 2 GA — no audio, supports enhancePrompt)

Workflow for creating YouTube Shorts:
1. For each scene, call generate_video_from_text with a detailed prompt OR generate_video_from_image with a source image and motion prompt
2. Prompts should describe subject, action, lighting, camera angle, and mood
3. Each clip is 4, 6, or 8 seconds. For a 30-60 second Short, generate 4-8 clips
4. After all clips are generated, call stitch_clips to combine them into one video

Aspect ratio:
- Vertical (Shorts): '9:16'
- Horizontal: '16:9'

Tips for best results:
- Use aspect_ratio '9:16' for vertical YouTube Shorts
- Default model is 'veo-3.1-fast-generate-001' (good balance of speed and quality)
- Keep prompts specific and cinematic — describe subject, action, lighting, camera angle, mood
- Add style keywords: 'cinematic', '4K', 'dramatic lighting', 'vertical composition'
- For Veo 3+ models, audio/dialogue is generated automatically (set generate_audio to true)
- Avoid complex multi-subject scenes
- The polling loop waits up to 5 minutes for completion
- Videos are returned as base64-encoded data and saved to temp files with accessible URLs

You can also use check_video_status to manually poll an operation that timed out.

tools.name: generate_video_from_text
tools.description: Generate a video from a text prompt using Google Gemini Veo. Polls until complete (up to 5 min). Models: veo-3.1-fast-generate-001 (default), veo-3.1-generate-001, veo-3.0-generate-001, veo-3.0-fast-generate-001, veo-2.0-generate-001. Aspect ratio: '16:9' (landscape) or '9:16' (portrait/Shorts).

prompt.description: Text description of the video to generate. Be specific about subject, action, lighting, camera angle, and mood.

duration.description: Video duration in seconds: 4, 6, or 8. Default: 8

generate_audio.description: Generate audio/dialogue in the video (Veo 3+ only). Default: true

negative_prompt.description: What to avoid in the generated video (optional)

tools.name: generate_video_from_image
tools.description: Generate a video from a source image (first frame) and optional text prompt using Google Gemini Veo. Polls until complete (up to 5 min). The image is used as the first frame and the model animates from it.

image_url.description: HTTPS URL of the source image to use as the first frame

prompt.description: Motion/direction prompt describing how the image should animate (e.g., 'slow zoom in, gentle camera pan right')

duration.description: Video duration in seconds: 4, 6, or 8. Default: 8

generate_audio.description: Generate audio in the video (Veo 3+ only). Default: true

tools.name: check_video_status
tools.description: Check the status of a Gemini Veo video generation operation without blocking

operation_name.description: The full operation name returned from a video generation request (e.g., 'models/veo-.../operations/...')

tools.name: stitch_clips
tools.description: Concatenate multiple video clips into a single video file using FFmpeg

clip_urls.description: Array of video clip URLs to concatenate in order

tools.handler:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Check FFmpeg is available
if ! command -v ffmpeg &> /dev/null; then
  echo '{"error": "FFmpeg is not installed. Please install FFmpeg to use clip stitching."}'
  exit 0
fi

# Parse clip URLs from environment variable (JSON array)
CLIP_URLS="$ARG_CLIP_URLS"
FORMAT="${ARG_OUTPUT_FORMAT:-mp4}"

# Create temp working directory
WORKDIR=$(mktemp -d /tmp/at-stitch-XXXXXX)
CONCAT_FILE="$WORKDIR/concat.txt"
OUTPUT_FILE="$WORKDIR/output.$FORMAT"

# Parse JSON array of URLs
URLS=$(echo "$CLIP_URLS" | python3 -c "import sys,json; urls=json.load(sys.stdin); [print(u) for u in urls]" 2>/dev/null || echo "$CLIP_URLS" | tr -d '[]"' | tr ',' '\n')

# Handle single clip case
COUNT=$(echo "$URLS" | wc -l)
if [ "$COUNT" -le 1 ]; then
  SINGLE_URL=$(echo "$URLS" | head -1 | tr -d ' ')
  echo "{\"output_url\": \"$SINGLE_URL\", \"clips_count\": 1, \"note\": \"Single clip returned as-is\"}"
  exit 0
fi

# Download each clip
IDX=0
while IFS= read -r url; do
  url=$(echo "$url" | tr -d ' ')
  [ -z "$url" ] && continue
  CLIP_FILE="$WORKDIR/clip_${IDX}.$FORMAT"
  curl -sL -o "$CLIP_FILE" "$url"
  echo "file '$CLIP_FILE'" >> "$CONCAT_FILE"
  IDX=$((IDX + 1))
done <<< "$URLS"

# Stitch clips
ffmpeg -y -f concat -safe 0 -i "$CONCAT_FILE" -c copy "$OUTPUT_FILE" 2>/dev/null

if [ -f "$OUTPUT_FILE" ]; then
  SIZE=$(stat -f%z "$OUTPUT_FILE" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$OUTPUT_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
  echo "{\"output_url\": \"file://$OUTPUT_FILE\", \"clips_count\": $IDX, \"file_size_bytes\": $SIZE, \"format\": \"$FORMAT\"}"
else
  echo '{"error": "FFmpeg stitching failed - no output file produced"}'
fi
